title: Village Lost to Time Reemerges as Greece Faces Historic Drought
bodyText: In the heart of Greece, the long-lost village of Kallio has resurfaced after decades beneath the waters of the Mornos reservoir, a result of an intense drought gripping the country. Sacrificed in the late 1970s to provide water to Athens, this once-thriving community now stands as a haunting reminder of what was lost—and what’s at stake as Greece battles an environmental crisis. This year, Greece recorded its hottest July on record, following an unusually mild winter. The combination of extreme heat and scarce rainfall has led to a severe water shortage across the country, straining resources and sparking widespread concern. The drought is a result of several interrelated factors: Relentless Heatwaves: This summer, a series of intense heatwaves have swept across Greece, pushing temperatures to record highs and placing immense pressure on the environment. Prolonged Dry Spells: For nearly three years, Greece has experienced scarce rainfall, exacerbating the drought conditions. Climate Change: The broader impacts of climate change are intensifying these weather patterns, making droughts more severe and prolonged. The changing climate is turning what were once rare events into a new normal. Overtourism: Adding to the pressure, Greece's booming tourist industry has further strained Greece's limited water resources, particularly in popular destinations where demand often exceeds supply. In response, Greek authorities are urging citizens to conserve water while tapping into additional reservoirs to mitigate the shortage. The situation highlights the delicate balance between human needs and natural resources and the urgent necessity for sustainable water management. The village of Kallio has reemerged for the second time since the early 1990s. When the Mornos Dam was built, nearly 80 homes, a church, and a school were submerged, displacing an entire community. The re-emergence of the village offers more than just a poignant look back in time; it serves as a quiet reminder of the long-term consequences of human intervention in the environment. Water Scarcity Crisis Resurfaces: Sunken Village of Kallio in Mornos Reappears Emerging Kallio, Mornos Lake Sunken village emerges as Greek drought bites (phys.org)
